question: How is PII/sensitive data governed?
resolution: Governance-by-disclosure: Discovery classifies data_sensitivity (tier0/tier1/tier2); Tier-2 = fixtures-only during build, real data connected post-handoff in the NGO's own environment; triage enforces.

question: What is the deliverable and who builds it?
resolution: A DEPLOYED app the non-technical NGO maintains via chat. Track A: Lovable is the durable home; Claude Code orchestrates via LovableDriver port; ai4good Skill is v1 CORE.
rejected: Repo-as-deliverable; Lovable as a side track

question: Can fuel be cashed out?
resolution: No cash-out in v1. Fuel is prepaid, non-cash-refundable, project-scoped credit; leftover becomes redeployable credit (keep or donate); move-between-projects is v1.5.

question: What legal entity?
resolution: Nonprofit path: fiscal sponsorship now, own 501(c)(3) later.

question: Unit economics for v1?
resolution: Blended sustainability: skim + grants + donations; v1 accepted as net-negative on skim alone; bottom-up year-1 P&L ordered.

question: Launch strategy?
resolution: Concierge-first: pre-recruited 20-30 volunteer bench, hand-match first 10-15 curated projects before organic browse. Supply liquidity is the #1 gate.

question: Legal framing of the no-guarantee posture?
resolution: Real counsel-reviewed ToS carrying no-warranty/no-SLA/limitation-of-liability; warm coordination-layer voice in UI.

question: Who consumes fuel and when can it be funded?
resolution: Unified fuel: NGO (Discovery, discovery_consumption kind) and volunteer (consumption) both draw project fuel at 15% skim; funding allowed from draft onward; email verification is the Discovery bot floor; NGO-doc verification is the publish wall.

question: Where does the PM task tree live?
resolution: SUPERSEDED BY d20. (Was: git-as-truth tasks.json via local TaskMaster + webhook projection.)

question: Task-tree simplification pass?
resolution: Six consolidations, ~-80h: one meter seam, one notify() comms substrate + EVENT_MAP, one outbox + idempotent handler registry with flat retry, defer concierge CRM + public-beta hardening slice.
rejected: Bespoke DLQ state machine; tuned backoff ladders

question: Credit model for the free Discovery layer?
resolution: Two-layer money: dollar-pegged fuel + abstract context-weighted Discovery credits; DAILY drip 10/day unverified, 30/day verified, hard reset 00:00 UTC, no rollover; 'Funded -> all-$' routing (positive-fuel projects bill Discovery to fuel); credits NGO-bound, fuel project-bound; honesty rule: funding removes the wall, never 'faster'.

question: Post-Discovery NGO chat?
resolution: REQ-033: funded projects unlock a read-only fuel-metered project assistant on the same chat surface (mode marker); never mutates; scope asks route to draft CRs.
rejected: New table/ledger kind; proactive digests in v1

question: Model for Discovery + assistant?
resolution: Opus (not Sonnet): highest-leverage reasoning step; credits are token-based/model-agnostic so allowance stays 10/30.

question: Spend-anomaly engine in v1?
resolution: Deferred to v1.5. Loss ceiling holds via deterministic caps + no cash-out + first-fund cap + fuel gate + daily human review of the money dashboard.

question: Project communication surface?
resolution: Lightweight project comment thread on task_comments (task_id NULL = project-level); system events -> notifications via notify(); full Slack-style channel deferred to v1.5.
rejected: Realtime channel tables/markdown/@-mentions in v1; full cut of NGO-volunteer free-text

question: CR distraction protection?
resolution: CR stays in_progress-only + guardrails: low-tone cr_raised (no email), one open CR per project (unique partial index), penalty-free Decline, NGO framing at submission. Only Accept creates work.
rejected: Broadening CR availability; real-time CR interrupts

question: Paused state in v1?
resolution: Removed: 10 lifecycle states; unpublish (open->scoped) is the only take-off-marketplace path; mid-build stops are support actions or cancel; pause/resume v1.5 on first real request.
rejected: paused state + clock-freeze stash columns + cron special-casing

question: Platform stack?
resolution: TanStack Start (SSR) on Lovable hosting + Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) + pg_cron; single repo, Lovable owns src/, Claude Code owns supabase/ and planning dirs; external Supabase, not Lovable Cloud.

question: Track B in v1?
resolution: Deferred post-v1: v1 is Track-A-only; non-A needs waitlisted at Discovery; Lovable mandatory (skip opt-out removed); 100% Lovable dependency accepted; enums reserved for no-migration reinstatement.
rejected: Building Track B with a weaker promise; rejecting non-A needs outright

question: Task system of record?
resolution: Linear (reverses d9) on real-signals grounds: free workspace per project; coordinator-owned decomposition (automation drafts, human approves, API push); pull-based self-assignment; status ONLY via GitHub integration on PR merge; detect-and-revert; linear-webhook -> one-writer Postgres projection; NGO sees panel only; CI tree snapshot at handoff; coordination infra platform-owned, never transfers.
rejected: git-as-truth tasks.json (agent-authored status violates real-signals); wrapper-MCP as primary (held in reserve)

question: Linear workspace creation (no public API) on the kickoff critical path?
resolution: Pool amendment: concierge batch pre-creates ready workspaces (API key + webhook + GitHub integration pre-connected); kickoff assigns via organizationUpdate rename, API-only; watermark linear_pool_replenish ops_task (default 3); pool-empty is the only stall mode. Fair-use note: bulk empty workspaces sharpen the need for Linear's written blessing.
rejected: Per-kickoff manual workspace creation (~5 min on the critical path)

question: Token governance for volunteer compute?
resolution: LLM gateway ships in v1 (pulls AI-Proxy-v1.5 forward): a4g_* virtual keys per volunteer x project (hash-only, show-once), caps (per-request + rolling 24h), fingerprint tripwire >~1.5k tokens, governance-prompt injection, inline per-request ledger writes (gateway_request_id idempotency), structural fuel gate, instant one-row revocation; real key only in gateway env; bodies never persisted; escalation ladder documented-not-built. Hosting = OD-6.
rejected: Per-project Anthropic workspaces + manual Console keys + usage poller + Cost-Report reconciliation + deactivation saga + multi-org router

question: Attribution and transparency scope for v1?
resolution: Capture in v1: REQ-034 task-ref binding on every ledger row (PostToolUse hook -> .a4g/task.md -> gateway extraction; floor unattributed, never gates; exploration/onboarding buckets; NGO burn-per-deliverable) + REQ-035 handoff attribution (testimonial + 3 credit-framed dimensions, supersedes no-satisfaction-form) + 30/60/90 jumpstart health with 60-day tried-and-failed confound question. Synthesis/matching surfaces v1.5.
rejected: Deferring capture (uncaptured data is lost forever); public star ratings (permanently out)
source: governance design thread + founder scope call

title: Single delivery model - Track B removed altogether
decision: Supersedes d19 deferral: track taxonomy deleted. One model: NGO-self-maintainable tool, Lovable durable home, Claude-Code-orchestrated build. Developer-grade waitlist deleted; needs failing the maintainability fit check are declined at Discovery with plain messaging, declines recorded for founder review (visibility only).
source: founder review comment on prd-new artifact

title: Review-batch 2 corrections
decision: (a) kickoff decomposition gated by coordinator review (one human gate, no admin ops tasks); (b) Skill ENFORCES pull-then-complete (explicit task-or-exploration binding before substantial work; completion = explicit dev act driving the merge); (c) handoff repo-transfer step removed (repo stays in platform org permanently); (d) Discovery file attachment/reads cost zero credits, no confirm interruption (daily allowance is the spend ceiling).
source: founder line-comments batch 2 on prd-new artifact

title: Dev-authored project PRD + automated completion gate
decision: Discovery output = scope contract, never decomposed directly. Kickoff seeds one bootstrap task: volunteer authors the project PRD from Discovery (clarifications via REQ-024 blockers). Automated scorer measures PRD completion vs Discovery; only >= threshold (OD-7, pilot-tuned) lets the project progress; build tree decomposed from the gated PRD (new REQ-036). Coordinator per-kickoff review retires to pilot spot-check (supersedes decomposition half of d24a). Scoring runs fuel-metered. Mirrors the ai4good buildout methodology itself.

title: MVP simplification pass (codex-debated)
decision: 12 cuts debated adversarially with codex (max 3 rounds each; all core cuts conceded, minimal guards forced): cadence minimal, badges->tags, cash-buffer alert->policy, canary/self-audit->v1.5 + org-settings guard, notif batching->v1.5 + atomic-outbox guard, blocker uniform rule + per-instance owner + Tier-2 warnings, observability=one money dashboard + all-surfaces metering audit invariant, runbooks 6->3 + hardened breach card, file-sync command->v1.5, money corrections = one privileged idempotent validating function (no UI). Founder verdicts folded: collusion detection REMOVED, admin credit grants REMOVED, AUP saga -> plain deactivate+revoke. PENDING founder confirm: c7 REQ-033 assistant deferral (reverses d12), c8 REQ-035 60/90 trim (trims d22).
source: founder MVP directive + codex adversarial debate wf_19ff9ac3-343

title: c7/c8 founder resolutions
decision: c7 REJECTED by founder: REQ-033 NGO assistant STAYS in v1 (decision-12 stands). c8 CONFIRMED: decision-22 amended - 60/90-day health layer + email to v1.5; v1 keeps 30-day reachability ping + attribution + structured day-45-60 founder check-in (required ops item at handoff acceptance, six-field PII-minimizing closed form, 2-business-day follow-up SLA).

title: Public-only v1; private repos removed
decision: Entire private-project feature dropped from v1 - all projects public MIT, no visibility choice, no Private badge, no private-repo justification/triage, no restricted project-page view. Confidential-codebase needs declined at Discovery (future feature). Orthogonal to data sensitivity: Tier-2 sensitive DATA still served via fixtures-only (public code, real data post-handoff). Retained as admin mechanics (not user features): org-namespace guard (member repos born private, flipped public after setup validation) + founder break-glass repo-hide (emergency takedown).
source: founder directive - drop the entire private thing, advanced feature for future

title: Second MVP-cut round (codex-evaluated, founder-ruled)
decision: s1 APPROVED-as-modified: organic marketplace (browse/filter/apply/applicant queue) to v1.5; v1 = concierge with ADMIN ENFORCE-MATCH (binding, no NGO approve/decline; volunteer one-click consent fires first-match disclaimer BEFORE any access; NGO per-match ack rides the funding screen); public read-only listings; admin match log feeds Goal 5. s2 APPROVED + founder extension: single-seat NGO accounts (invites/roles v1.5; authority attestation, no shared credentials, audited contact-transfer, escalation contact) AND single-dev projects (one volunteer per project, teams post-v1). s4 APPROVED-total-removal: donation flow gone from PRD entirely; leftover credit auto-applies at any funding checkout (can satisfy $50 min); release = ledger op under control totals. s3 REJECTED (EU/UK VAT + Stripe Tax stay v1). s5 REJECTED (Google OAuth stays).
source: founder rulings on codex-evaluated suggestions wf_578fddfe-1bf

title: Third MVP round + flow verdicts
decision: Flow #7 audited-reversal req REMOVED; #8 handoff-dispute review REMOVED. r1 APPROVED: structured CRs -> v1.5 (amends d16; informal comment-thread protocol, task-before-spend, top-priority-or-acknowledged, one-discussion rule, sensitivity re-triage). r2 APPROVED: public profile+badge -> v1.5 (append-only credit events, private handoff confirmation, honest copy). r3 APPROVED: verification machinery -> ONE audited founder-vetted action (no public verified claim, public-evidence-preferred PII rule, v1.5 gate before non-concierge admission). r4 FOUNDER REDESIGN: automated triage screener - confident-clean auto-approves + auto audit record; Tier-2 never auto; non-decided -> founder exception queue (return-to-scoped vs terminal-decline); daily-review spot-check; OD-8 threshold. r5 REJECTED: in-app notification center STAYS in v1.
source: founder one-by-one rulings, codex round wf_f9edfc79-fab

title: Provider truth drives gauges, tight reconciliation + flow 9 removal
decision: Fuel gauge = Anthropic-reported usage per gateway response (real-time by construction); aggregate reconciliation vs Anthropic usage/cost reporting on tight pull (minutes, pilot-tuned) instead of nightly; internal control totals stay nightly. Lovable meter: v1 manual widget + consent session reads (no API - Q7); STANDING TRIGGER to switch to pulled provider data when Lovable exposes usage programmatically. Flow #9 goodwill refund REMOVED ENTIRELY - zero refunds in v1, wronged NGOs made whole in spendable credit, only money-out = Stripe dispute rail.

title: Fully automatic ledger corrections (flow #10 removed)
decision: No human correction step. Tight-cadence reconciliation auto-conforms ledger to provider truth: deterministic hierarchy (Stripe wins money-in; Anthropic wins AI spend; pairing arithmetic resolves internal gaps); corrections auto-post via the guarded idempotent function (audit rows, dashboard visibility, large drift pages AFTER applying). Backstop: undecidable drift (provider data missing/contradictory) never guessed - books untouched, pages for visibility. Supersedes d26/c12 admin-invoked procedure.

title: Manual-flows session closed - all 25 resolved
decision: APPROVED backstops: #3 chargeback adjudication, #12 discovery-failure escalation, #13 DLQ, #14 incident command (steps scripted one-click), #15 blocker 7d rung. REMOVED: #2,#4,#5,#7,#8,#9,#10 (+#1 resolved by d25 redesign). MOOT: #6 (d27 public-only). POSTPONED later-stage: #11 reserve, #16 DMCA docs (CSAM statutory duty remains by law), #17 counsel (gates public launch; pilot on draft ToS), #18 Anthropic negotiation, #19 P&L cadence, #20 Linear blessing (gates pool scale-out), #23 extra runbooks. FIRE-AT-MOMENT: #21 ODs, #22 launch setup, #24 pool VERIFY, #25 pilot runs.

title: d32 scope correction
decision: Founder postponed ONLY #16 (DMCA/CSAM docs), not the #17-#25 batch. Counsel-timing and Linear-blessing edits REVERTED in prd-new; #17-#25 remain open for one-by-one rulings.

title: Flow session paused at #21
decision: #17 counsel, #18 Anthropic negotiation, #19 P&L cadence, #20 Linear blessing - ALL POSTPONED post-MVP. #21 OD register through #25 pilot runs remain open; session paused by founder for a full MVP-PRD review pass.

title: Handoff reframed as access-transition, not delivery
decision: Under enforced git-bound Lovable (d23), the live app + repo are the NGO's throughout the build - so handoff delivers/transfers NOTHING. Handoff = (1) access change: NGO removes the dev or drops to read-only (write ends, keys terminate, Linear membership removed, NGO removes from Lovable workspace) + (2) bookkeeping (attribution, completion credit, fuel release, ->handed_off). Quality checklist is a completion confirmation, not a delivery gate (app already live). Story 6 + REQ-012 reframed.

title: Discovery scope guardrails (no drip of unrelated execution)
decision: Discovery + REQ-033 assistant scope-guard conversations on free credits AND funded fuel: (1) platform-side system-prompt scope line - decline unrelated tasks (general Q&A, drafting, translation, coding help), redirect to scoping - mirrors the gateway governance line; (2) deterministic per-conversation turn ceiling (platform-configurable, pilot-tuned) - past it, wrap-up + generate-scope-or-start-fresh; (3) repeated off-topic declines -> plain NGO notice + founder-visibility flag in daily review. Visibility, never lockout.

title: d34 scope corrected: FREE PHASE ONLY
decision: Founder ruling: scope guardrails (prompt scope line, turn ceiling, off-topic visibility flag) apply ONLY while Discovery runs on free daily credits - they protect the platform subsidy. Funded Discovery and the REQ-033 assistant carry NO scope guardrail: paid usage is the NGO's own call; the per-turn cost display + fuel gauge are the controls.

title: Platform Lovable member seat; offboarding automated
decision: Setup checklist adds one invite: platform ops account joins each Lovable workspace as build-phase member. Seat powers automated volunteer removal at handoff/abandonment/AUP deactivation; at handoff the platform removes itself (last one out - zero post-handoff access, when real NGO data lives in Lovable; build-phase exposure bounded by Tier-2 fixtures-only). Narrow exception to no-Lovable-access posture: member management only, no usage/billing reads. VERIFY on first pilot: member add/remove/self-remove via Lovable API/MCP; fallback = manual NGO removal with notice. Risk-table mitigation updated.

title: Volunteer candidacy precedes matching; GitHub link mandatory at signup
decision: Amends d28: volunteers mark interest in-product (candidate for this project) on public listings; each open project gets an assimilation window (platform-configurable, pilot-tuned) to gather candidates before the concierge enforce-match draws from the accumulated pool. Candidacies feed the admin match log only - no NGO-facing queue, NGO never sees candidacies. REQ-011 listings gain exactly one volunteer action (mark interest); otherwise still read-only. GitHub link becomes mandatory at volunteer signup (supersedes d28 at-first-consent timing; no unlinked volunteer accounts); platform GitHub-org membership still waits for first match consent so repo-creation rights are never granted to never-matched volunteers.
source: founder review comment on REQ-007 (halved prd-mvp artifact)

title: Assimilation window = time-based, clock starts at first candidacy
decision: Founder ruling: the d36 assimilation window is TIME-based, not count-based, and its clock starts at the FIRST candidacy. A candidate-less project just stays open (Goal-5 unmatched-aging still applies); the first candidate starts the countdown; later candidates join the pool without resetting the clock; at lapse the concierge matches from the accumulated pool. Duration platform-configurable, pilot-tuned.

title: Assimilation window backs off per candidate - interest expedites matching
decision: Founder ruling: each additional candidacy SHRINKS the remaining assimilation window (backoff factor pilot-tuned). Accumulating interest expedites the match and never extends the wait; strong interest converges toward near-immediate concierge matching. The clock still starts at the first candidacy; candidate-less projects stay open under Goal-5 aging.

title: NGO-only bot interface on the project page
decision: The project page stays publicly readable and role-uniform in its content (task tree, cadence, activity), but the interactive assistant/bot (REQ-033) is surfaced ONLY to the project's NGO account - never to the assigned volunteer, platform visitors, or the public. Rationale: the assistant spends the NGO's fuel and answers the NGO's status questions; volunteers and the public get the page content, not a bot. Amends REQ-010 (adds the one viewer-specific element) and REQ-033 (NGO-only surfacing).
source: founder review comment on REQ-010 (pure prd-mvp artifact)

title: GitHub-style project activity UI; cadence = task progression + commits
decision: Fold in a GitHub-repo-view-style activity UI for the project page + in-progress showcase card (languages/stack bar, updated-N-ago, activity sparkline, contributor, license, handoff-readiness %, cause tags). CADENCE REDEFINED: liveness is measured from BOTH PM task progression (Linear task-completion events) AND commit activity, not commits alone. Open-project listing card stays static attributes only (no repo yet). Popularity metrics (stars/forks/watchers) are NEVER shown - reputation is completion credit, not popularity (Promise 5, Out-of-Scope 11), and interest/candidacy is admin-only (d36). Amends REQ-010 (cadence + repo-derived activity signals) + REQ-011 (open card fields + in-progress showcase). UI-mimic design detail in architecture-notes Cross-cutting C.
source: founder review comment on REQ-011 + research request (mimic GitHub UI)

title: Workspace-backed money model: metering out of the gateway, into Anthropic per-project workspaces
decision: Adopt the hybrid workspace-backed money path (partially reverses d21). Each project is isolated in its own Anthropic Workspace (created via the Admin API at kickoff, archived at handoff/cancellation, retained through abandonment/rematch so the successor inherits it). The per-project MONEY-OF-RECORD becomes Anthropic's billed cost per workspace (Usage/Cost API group_by=workspace_id) - NOT reconstructed from local token math; the 15% margin is recognized on that billed cost. The prepaid fuel maps to the workspace's provider spend limit (raised on top-up), so the hard fuel ceiling is provider-enforced. The GATEWAY IS RETAINED for its other roles - key confinement (volunteer never sees the workspace key), governance-prompt injection, project-binding tripwire, and the real-time zero-fuel gate trigger - but its inline token estimate is demoted to a live preview + gate trigger, never the ledger. This resolves the fragility of token-count metering (founder: token calc against the meter is fragile). 100-workspaces/org cap is not a blocker: archive-on-handoff (archived don't count) makes it a concurrent-in-flight limit; create new Anthropic orgs if approached; negotiate a cap lift once the platform gains traction. Touches REQ-009 (money path + workspace isolation + fuel gate), REQ-006 (ledger anchor now per-project), REQ-005.5 (kickoff provisions workspace), REQ-012 (handoff archives workspace), NFR workspace-cap note un-retired, Out-of-Scope #20 (provisioning now in v1) + #22 (Anthropic-side budget enforcement now used). architecture-notes Cross-cutting A (new workspace layer) + B (anchor per-project).

title: Provider-native caps suffice: retire custom gateway usage caps + OD-3
decision: Founder: the provider cap and rate limit suffice at this stage. Drop the gateway's custom per-key usage caps (per-request max + rolling-24h window sized 3-4x a heavy human day + 80/100% soft/hard + coordinator override). Budget ceiling = the Anthropic workspace SPEND LIMIT (= prepaid fuel, provider-enforced, d39); velocity/runaway = the workspace RATE LIMITS (RPM/TPM, provider-enforced). v1 adds no custom gateway caps; a light gateway anomaly-brake can return later if needed. OD-3 (volunteer spend-cap + low-fuel warning levels) RETIRED - spend-cap resolved to provider-native; the 20/5/0% warning levels are already fixed. Threat-posture line updated (spend limit bounds exposure, rate limits bound velocity). Touches REQ-009 caps bullet + threat posture, s9 OD register (OD-3 removed), architecture-notes Cross-cutting A layer 3 + tuning list.

title: Fuel state + thresholds read from Anthropic Admin API (single source of truth); provider enforces the zero-fuel stop, gateway proxies
decision: Founder: the 20/5/0% thresholds + notifications are a platform tool that reads the single source of truth = the Anthropic Admin/usage API (per-workspace spend vs limit). The gateway does NOT fail requests at 0% - Anthropic's workspace spend limit declines them and the gateway just proxies the rejection to the user. Removes the gateway's role as fuel-gate enforcer (reverses the d39 wording 'gateway inline as immediate path / gate trigger'). Gateway per-request usage capture survives ONLY as attribution telemetry (REQ-034), never the money ledger and never the gate. Fixes the Story-4 'never polling-based' contradiction (the monitor DOES poll the Admin API on a tight cadence). Touches REQ-009 (opening pipeline, money path, fuel thresholds, 401 semantics), REQ-006 (ledger = provider-truth single source + provider-enforced ceiling), Story 4 (s2), architecture-notes Cross-cutting A layer 5 + B.
source: founder review comment on REQ-009 fuel thresholds

title: Enforcement-placement wording aligned with d41 (two platform-controlled surfaces)
decision: Founder question surfaced imprecision: REQ-009 said durable enforcement lives in the gateway, but post-d41 the fuel/spend/rate enforcement moved to the Anthropic workspace. Clarified: the principle (policy cannot live in volunteer-editable files or third-party RBAC) holds, but there are now TWO platform-controlled surfaces - the GATEWAY (key confinement, binding tripwire, governance-prompt injection) and the PROVIDER-WORKSPACE LIMITS the platform sets via the Admin API (spend + rate, enforced provider-side but platform-configured, volunteer cannot override). Touches REQ-009 enforcement-placement paragraph + architecture-notes placement doctrine.
source: founder question on REQ-009 enforcement placement

title: Pull Lovable credit status via the Lovable MCP (get_workspace); RM-58 standing trigger met
decision: Research: the Lovable MCP get_workspace returns plan + available credits, and the Lovable MCP is now a standard offering across all plan tiers (no longer labeled Research Preview), OAuth-authenticated. So the RM-58/decision-30 standing trigger (switch from manual to pulled when Lovable exposes programmatic usage) is MET. FOLD: Lovable credit status is read programmatically from Lovable via the volunteer's connected Lovable session (client-side, consent-gated - Q7 path, no new access, no decision-35 change), with a manual report as FALLBACK (Lovable MCP has no documented SLA/rate limit). ai4good computes + surfaces low/exhausted in-platform and notifies the NGO. Top-up stays a Lovable billing action (deep-link; may suggest Lovable auto-top-up) - no programmatic purchase, no Lovable-native credit webhook. DEFERRED: a background/standing poll for between-session alerts would need the platform ops seat to hold its own Lovable OAuth + read billing, widening decision-35's member-management-only scope. Touches REQ-021 (status pulled + AC), REQ-010 (standing trigger now met), REQ-024 (blocker type), Q7 (s9); architecture-notes REQ-021.
source: founder research request on Lovable credit comms + correction that the MCP is no longer Research Preview

title: Lovable MCP automation map: automate kickoff provisioning, governance-into-Lovable, handoff live-URL capture; member-mgmt not available (manual offboarding)
decision: Studied the full Lovable MCP surface and mapped it to current workflows. FOLDED automation wins (all run in the volunteer connected session, no new access): (a) kickoff auto-provisions the Lovable project (create_project) + its database (enable_database) instead of a manual checklist; (b) governance-into-Lovable - ai4good injects its conventions + reviewer skill into the Lovable project via set_project_knowledge/create_workspace_skill so Lovable own agent follows them (closes the ungoverned-Lovable-agent gap, soft-norm); (c) handoff live deployment URL captured automatically via deploy_project (manual paste fallback), + get_project screenshot for showcase/review; (d) build review via get_diff/list_files/read_file; DB-state verifiable via get_database_status/query_database. CANNOT do (stay manual/UI): add_connector is UI-only (GitHub/Stripe connectors stay dashboard steps); NO member-management tools -> automated offboarding NOT possible (resolves the old [VERIFY]) -> NGO manually removes the volunteer AND the platform ops seat at handoff (the seat also cannot self-remove); workspace creation + credit top-up stay NGO-side. TWO OPEN DECISIONS flagged (not folded): (1) post-handoff 30-day health via Lovable analytics (get_project_analytics/trend) -> real adoption signal but needs a persistent platform Lovable connection (widens Q7/seat scope); (2) platform ops seat fate (revisits d35) - its offboarding purpose is unachievable via MCP; drop it or keep it only to hold the platform OAuth for #1. Touches REQ-021 (setup auto-provision + governance + offboarding), REQ-012 (URL auto-capture), REQ-021 ACs; architecture-notes Cross-cutting D.
source: founder request to study the Lovable MCP doc for automation opportunities

title: Lovable MCP automation map VERIFIED against the live API (endpoints called directly)
decision: Interrogated the connected Lovable MCP directly (read-only calls on the founder account: get_me, list_workspaces, get_workspace, list_connectors, list_available_connectors, list_template_projects, list_projects, get_workspace_knowledge, list_workspace_skills). Confirms the d43 automation map with real responses, and REFINES: (1) get_workspace returns billing-period USAGE + limits (backend_total_used_in_billing_period, billing_period_credits_limit, per-member monthly_credit_limit), not a single balance - ai4good computes remaining/low from usage-vs-limit; the native per-member monthly_credit_limit maps to ai4good per-task/volunteer Lovable cap (UI-set only). (2) list_projects/get_project return url (published live URL), preview_url, editor_url, status, latest_screenshot_url - handoff URL auto-capture + showcase screenshot are real fields. (3) create_project tech_stack supports the ai4good target (tanstack_start_ts_*, live on the account existing AI4GOOD project) + design_systems/template_project_id, but list_template_projects is empty -> ai4good should maintain an ai4good-starter template + design system for consistent per-project scaffolding. (4) workspace knowledge empty + skills none -> governance-into-Lovable (set_workspace_knowledge/create_workspace_skill) is free to populate. (5) connector catalog: zero-config lovable-cloud/supabase/stripe/paddle/shopify/ai_gateway, standard github(API)/linear/etc, mcp linear/notion/sentry/posthog/etc; add_connector still UI-only (deep-link); gitsync_github is a workspace feature. No PRD requirement changed (all requirements verified feasible); refinements recorded in architecture-notes Cross-cutting D.
source: founder: install the MCP and actually interrogate its exposed api

title: No post-handoff Lovable analytics at this stage; 30-day check stays reachability-only
decision: Founder: post-handoff analytics is not required at this stage. Resolves d43 open decision #1 = NO. REQ-012 30-day-alive stays reachability-only - a plain request to the public live lovable.app URL, which needs NO Lovable API access and no persistent platform Lovable connection. Lovable get_project_analytics/trend are not used in v1. Consequence (recommended, pending founder confirmation): the platform ops Lovable member seat (d35) now has no remaining purpose - offboarding is not MCP-automatable (d43) and analytics is not required - so it should be DROPPED; then ai4good is never a member of the NGO Lovable workspace, offboarding is the NGO removing the volunteer only, and last-one-out is automatic. Seat-drop touches REQ-021/007/005.5/027 + s9 risk and revises d35 - flagged for explicit founder go before folding.
source: founder: no post handoff analytics is required at this stage

title: Handoff workflow DROPPED from MVP; replaced by lean Project Completion; platform Lovable seat dropped
decision: Founder: drop the entire handoff thing outside of MVP PRD. REQ-012 rewritten Handoff Workflow -> Project Completion: a project completes when all P0 tasks are done; because Lovable is enforced and git-bound the NGO already owns the live app + repo throughout (no delivery, no transfer) so there is NO formal handoff ceremony in v1 - the completion checklist gate, sign-off/acceptance flow, guided-maintenance ritual, rejection loop, live-URL gate, and post-completion attribution+health are all deferred (new RM-62). On completion: leftover fuel -> general balance; virtual keys revoked + provider workspace archived; Linear membership removed + tree preserved; completion-credit + first-tool badge; NGO offboards the volunteer self-serve. Lifecycle collapses 10 states -> 9: handoff_pending + handed_off REPLACED by single `completed` (in_progress -> completed). Founder forks: Goal 1 metric = 'Projects reaching completion (all P0 tasks done)' with NO live-deployment condition; the 30-day-alive check is DROPPED entirely (deferred). REQ-035 (post-handoff attribution & health) reduced to a deferred stub -> RM-62; roadmap RM-40 (reputation-feeds-matching) + RM-41 (AI-maintainability check) folded into RM-62 (they were REQ-035 sub-points, orphaned by the rewrite). Also FOLDED (confirmed 'Yes', from d44): platform ops Lovable member seat DROPPED everywhere (REQ-021 setup/completion/AC/non-goals, s9 risk, Story 6) - ai4good is never a member of the NGO Lovable workspace, offboarding = NGO removes the volunteer only; revises d35. Edits span all 9 pure-s* section files + roadmap.md (RM-62 added, RM-40/41 removed) + architecture-notes.md (REQ-012/035 sections tagged deferred, seat decision #2 resolved DROPPED, handoff->completion wording). Assembled prd-mvp.md verify: 16475 words, 37 REQ headings, 60/60 RM bijection, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned words, 0 FFFD.
source: founder: Yes and drop the entire handoff thing outside of MVP prd

title: Lovable onboarding: NGO owns workspace+billing and invites the volunteer as a build-only Editor (not admin)
decision: Resolves the Lovable NGO-onboarding thread. Model: the NGO owns the Lovable workspace and its billing/subscription and invites the volunteer directly; the volunteer is a build-only member (Lovable Editor), NOT workspace admin. Fixes REQ-021's prior wording 'invites the volunteer as workspace admin'. Verified against Lovable's live role matrix (2026-07-12): billing (Plans & credit usage - top-up, plan, payment method) is owner/admin-only ('all other roles see the tab but every billing control is disabled and cannot initiate or approve charges'); an Editor CAN Manage GitHub (connect two-way sync) and Publish, plus build/edit/set project knowledge; an Editor CANNOT Disconnect Supabase (DB connection = admin/owner), Manage Integrations (workspace connectors = admin/owner), or access billing. So kickoff splits by tier: NGO owner does billing + admin-gated setup (create workspace, enable database, add connectors, set the volunteer's per-member monthly_credit_limit = native Lovable spend cap, invite the volunteer as Editor); volunteer (Editor) provisions the project, connects GitHub (repo in platform org), injects conventions + reviewer skill, builds, publishes, consumes credits - never touches billing. Volunteer's per-member credit cap is the native volunteer-Lovable-spend control (UI-set). REJECTED alternatives: (C) platform service seat doing setup - needs a real ai4good Lovable bot account, reverses d45, and billing-owner ambiguity if the platform creates the workspace; (B) volunteer transient-admin with card-added-last sequencing - founder judged it cumbersome. d45 unchanged: no platform seat, ai4good is never a member. OPEN ITEMS (in architecture-notes, not blocking): [verify] whether the INITIAL enable-Lovable-Cloud-database toggle is editor-blocked (matrix confirms Disconnect Supabase = admin; initial enable unconfirmed) and whether an Editor can create_project - 5-min test with a second Editor account; if both editor-allowed, the volunteer does ALL setup and the NGO's only technical step disappears. [open] repo location: REQ-008 puts the repo in the platform GitHub org, which requires the volunteer to hold create/connect rights in that org (Lovable GitHub-App install is org-admin); alternative = repo in the NGO's own GitHub removes that dependency but moves the public-only/visibility guard to per-repo checks - does not change REQ-008 yet. Folded into REQ-021 (setup sequence + billing-isolation requirement + AC) and architecture-notes REQ-021 (role matrix + kickoff ordering + the two open items).
source: founder Lovable-onboarding thread: 'No I prefer it to be the ngo invites the volunteer' + 'Yes' to fold the Editor-based flow

title: Editor-role test resolved: volunteer does the ENTIRE Lovable technical setup; NGO role collapses to account/billing
decision: Ran the d46 [verify] via Lovable's authoritative full workspace role matrix (docs.lovable.dev/features/collaboration). Result: Editor CAN 'Create new projects in a workspace' (Yes) and 'Manage Supabase' = enable/manage the database (Yes); only the destructive 'Disconnect Supabase' is admin/owner. Also Editor: Manage GitHub = Yes, Publish = Yes; and Editor CANNOT: 'Manage plans & billing' (No - billing locked, as wanted), 'Manage Integrations' (No - workspace-level connectors admin/owner), 'View usage' (No). Consequence: the volunteer (Editor) does the ENTIRE technical setup - create project, enable DB, connect GitHub, inject conventions, build, publish - and the NGO's technical step DISAPPEARS: the NGO only creates the workspace, funds it, sets the volunteer's per-member credit cap, and invites the volunteer as Editor (plus adds a workspace-level connector only if a specific project needs one, e.g. Stripe). REQ-021 setup line updated to move DB-enable + project creation from the NGO back to the volunteer. Refines d46 (which had provisionally parked DB-enable with the NGO owner pending this test). TWO CAVEATS surfaced by the matrix, logged in architecture-notes: (1) [verify] 'View usage' = Editor No -> the volunteer's editor session may be UNABLE to read the workspace credit balance via get_workspace, which REQ-021/d42 assume; needs a live get_workspace-as-editor check; if blocked, credit-status read moves to the NGO/owner session or the manual-report fallback already in REQ-021. (2) Manage Integrations = editor No -> workspace-level connectors stay owner-side. Note: this is a documented-matrix result, not a live editor-session test (the MCP runs as the founder = owner, so an editor session cannot be simulated headlessly); the one remaining live check is the credit-read caveat.

title: Live editor-session test: non-owner member get_workspace returns full usage -> credit-read caveat RESOLVED
decision: Ran the d47 credit-read [verify] live. Founder added an editor-role user (sharon.drang@gmail.com) to Nir's Lovable workspace and re-authenticated the Lovable MCP as that account; get_me confirmed the session identity (email sharon.drang@gmail.com, role 'member' on Nir's Lovable). The editor/member's get_workspace returned the FULL usage/billing payload identical to the owner's: backend_total_used_in_billing_period=1.19, billing_period_credits_used/_limit, period start/end dates, plan=ktlo_2, subscription_status=active, payment_provider=stripe, with membership.role='member' proving the non-owner token. CONCLUSION: the role-matrix row 'View usage = No' for Editor gates only the billing dashboard/management UI, NOT the get_workspace API read - a non-owner member reads credit/usage fine. So the volunteer's own connected session can read the Lovable credit balance for ai4good's UI (the d42 design and REQ-021 line 'credit status read via the volunteer's session' both HOLD, unchanged); no NGO-owner OAuth session and no manual fallback are required for the read. No pure-doc (prd-mvp) change; architecture-notes REQ-021 credit-read item flipped [verify]->[resolved]. Naming nuance: Lovable's API labels the editor-level role as 'member'. Only remaining Lovable-setup open item is the repo-location decision (platform org vs NGO GitHub). Note: MCP is now authenticated as the editor account - re-auth back to owner (nirdrang) when needed.
source: founder ran option 2: re-authed the Lovable MCP as the editor account for a live get_workspace test

title: Live-confirmed: editor (non-owner member) can create_project + enable_database via MCP
decision: Completed the empirical validation of the editor-role setup (hardening d47's matrix claims). Still authenticated as the editor account (sharon.drang@gmail.com, role member on Nir's Lovable), ran two side-effecting MCP calls: (1) create_project(workspace_id=Nir's Lovable, minimal prompt, wait=false) -> SUCCEEDED, returned projectId 36fab6b5-0163-4cad-bf8e-5e9d41ffb88c (status in_progress) => an editor CAN create projects. (2) enable_database(project 36fab6b5...) -> SUCCEEDED, returned {enabled:true, stack:supabase} => an editor CAN provision the cloud database. Combined with d48 (editor get_workspace returns full usage) this LIVE-confirms the entire editor-does-all-setup flow for everything MCP-testable: create project + enable DB + read credit balance. Publish corroborated (not separately run) by the workspace field external_publish_permission_level=member. GitHub connect + billing-lock remain matrix-only (GitHub connect is a UI step not MCP-callable; there is no billing MCP tool). CLEANUP NOTE: the Lovable MCP exposes NO delete/archive-project tool -> the test project 36fab6b5... + its Supabase DB persist in Nir's Lovable and must be deleted from the Lovable dashboard (editor_url https://lovable.dev/projects/36fab6b5-0163-4cad-bf8e-5e9d41ffb88c). Also: MCP still authenticated as the editor account - re-auth to owner (nirdrang) when needed. No pure-doc change; architecture-notes REQ-021 resolved-note upgraded matrix->live-tested.

title: Server-side Lovable credit reader via headless Claude Code + ai4good editor bot (INTENDED, conditional on token-persistence proof)
decision: Founder rejected the volunteer-in-session credit read as fragile/dev-machine-dependent and chose a platform server-side reader. Design: a dedicated ai4good BOT Lovable account is an EDITOR member of each NGO workspace; a SERVER runs headless Claude Code with the Lovable MCP authenticated once (browser) as the bot; a SKILL loops get_workspace over the active-project workspace_id list, computes OK/low/exhausted, and POSTs to an ai4good edge function then to a per-project cache then the UI (REQ-010). KEY UNLOCK: a custom server cannot auth to Lovable (OAuth-only, whitelisted clients, no API keys/service accounts - verified: 'other MCP clients cannot complete the OAuth flow'), BUT Claude Code IS a whitelisted client, so headless Claude Code is a supported client rather than a blocked custom server. Editor role keeps billing locked (editor can read get_workspace/create_project/enable_database per d47-d49, cannot touch billing). INVITE CHAIN B: NGO invites only the volunteer; the volunteer (editor can Add Editor) invites the bot, so the NGO stays at ONE invite; offboarding removes both (editor can Remove Editor). CONDITIONALITY (founder: 'if this works its great'): the make-or-break verify is headless OAuth PERSISTENCE - does Claude Code hold and refresh the bot token long-term without human re-auth? Lovable documents no refresh/headless guarantee, so it must be proven empirically (auth once, confirm get_workspace still works days/weeks later) plus monitoring, auth-failure alert, and a re-auth runbook. If it fails, fall back to volunteer in-session read or reduce-the-need (plan-sizing + auto-refill + Lovable low-credit emails). REVERSES d45 (ai4good never a member) and Q7 (no standing platform access), scoped to a read-only editor bot; blast radius = one token reads every joined workspace, so editor-only, dedicated account, secure token storage, rotation, shard at scale. PURE DOC INTENTIONALLY NOT CHANGED YET (frozen pending proof): when adopted, REQ-021 'read via the volunteers session' becomes mechanism-neutral, 'ai4good is never a member' becomes 'read-only editor monitoring bot removed at completion', offboarding removes bot+volunteer, and a new RM (durable Lovable service-account/API-key) referenced from the pure doc to keep the RM bijection. PARALLEL: raise the API-key/service-account ask with Lovable. Captured in architecture-notes REQ-021; no prd-mvp or roadmap change this commit.
source: founder: server with claude code running authenticate with the ai4good seat wire a skill to pull lovable credits; If this works its great and should be captured

title: NGO performs BOTH Lovable invites (read-only bot + volunteer); both memberships are a hard kickoff gate on volunteer start + state progression
decision: Refines the d50 bot-reader onboarding. The NGO performs BOTH invites at kickoff: the ai4good read-only bot (editor) AND the volunteer (editor). Reverses the earlier chain-B (volunteer invites bot) and the operator-invites-volunteer idea. WHY the NGO does both: the headless bot has no way to invite anyone - the Lovable MCP has no member-management verb (inviting is dashboard-only); an editor ACCOUNT has the Add Editor permission but only a human at the dashboard can exercise it, and the bot is headless. So all invites are human-at-dashboard actions, and the founder assigns them to the NGO. GATE: both memberships are a HARD kickoff gate - the volunteer cannot start working and project state cannot progress to active build until both are confirmed - so the platform is NEVER building blind to Lovable credit consumption + workspace state (the read-only monitoring bot is provably present before any credits are spent). Folds into the existing REQ-024 'Lovable setup pending' blocker + REQ-005.5 kickoff gate. CONFIRMATION: the bot self-confirms its own membership via a successful get_workspace; the volunteer's membership is confirmed via their own session / validated paste-back ([verify] - there is no member-list API to enumerate members). RATIONALE / least-privilege: the read-only editor bot BOUNDS the platform's Lovable visibility to exactly credit-consumption + state, nothing more, and the NGO controls admission. Offboarding removes both (dashboard). A Lovable member-management API would remove both manual invites -> bundled into the durable Lovable ask alongside service-account/API-key + headless auth. STILL CONDITIONAL on d50's token-persistence proof; PURE DOC UNCHANGED (frozen). Pending pure-doc edits when d50 adopted: REQ-021 setup = NGO invites BOTH (bot + volunteer) + gate; REQ-024 'Lovable setup pending' covers both invites; REQ-005.5 gates state progression on both memberships. Captured in architecture-notes REQ-021 (d50 block); no prd-mvp or roadmap change.
source: founder: 'lets make sure that the ngo do the two invites. This should gate continue development and state progression for volunteer to start working since this will reduce the platform visibility to loveable workspace credit consumption and state'

title: Scrub false 'ai4good is never a member' leftovers from the MVP PRD; fold the read-only monitoring-account membership (d51) into the pure doc
decision: Founder review caught that REQ-012's offboarding line ('ai4good is never a member of the NGO's Lovable workspace, so there is nothing for the platform to remove') is now FALSE, because d50/d51 make the ai4good read-only monitoring bot a member. Directed to scrub all leftovers from the MVP PRD + artifact. Scrubbed 8 spots across the pure-s* sources: REQ-008 completion, REQ-010 credit-source, REQ-012 offboarding (the quoted line), REQ-021 setup + credit-read + offboarding + AC + non-goals, REQ-005.5 lifecycle, Story 6 (headline + detail), s9 risk row. The PRD now consistently states: ai4good runs a READ-ONLY MONITORING ACCOUNT (editor role, no billing/admin) in each NGO Lovable workspace that surfaces credit consumption + workspace state; the NGO invites BOTH the volunteer and the monitoring account at kickoff, BOTH validated before the volunteer starts (the d51 gate, folded); offboarding removes BOTH; credit status is read via the monitoring account (was 'via the volunteer's connected session'); non-goal fixed (was 'no backend access / credit visibility client-side' -> now 'read-only via the monitoring account; no Lovable billing control; no per-task metering'). SCOPE NOTE: this folds the MEMBERSHIP + invites + gate + read-source (the firm d51 decision) into the pure requirements doc. The read MECHANISM (headless Claude Code as the monitoring account) stays in architecture-notes and remains CONDITIONAL on the token-persistence proof; if that proof fails and the bot is dropped, these membership statements revert. Reassembled prd-mvp: 16632 words, 37 REQ, 60/60 RM bijection, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned words, 0 FFFD. Artifact re-injected + republished. Still pending: a roadmap RM for the durable Lovable service-account/API-key + member-management API (referenced from the pure doc when added).
source: founder review comment on REQ-012: 'This is false. Make sure the artifact and the MVP prd are scrubbed from left overs'

title: Strip REQ-026 Linear-provisioning pool-model HOW from the PRD (pure-requirements discipline); mechanism already in architecture-notes
decision: Founder review: the REQ-026 'Provisioning - pool model' paragraph is implementation design, not a PRD-level item ('not the details of the how to do it'). Stripped the pool HOW from 5 pure-doc spots and kept the requirement-level WHAT: REQ-026 provisioning paragraph (now: each project is assigned its Linear workspace at kickoff with no manual step on the critical path; volunteer invited + PRD bootstrap seeded REQ-036; provisioning that cannot complete surfaces as an external-dependency blocker, never silently stalling or inventing a sub-state; pilot on Linear free tier, RM-30); REQ-026 AC (was 'pool assignment + pre-empty replenishment' -> 'workspace assigned at kickoff, no manual step; if none, blocker'); REQ-026 removed the '[VERIFY on the first pool batch]' AC entirely (pure design-validation, already in architecture-notes); REQ-005.5 kickoff ('assigned from the pre-created pool' -> 'assigned (unavailability raises ops task + blocker)'); admin-work-items line ('pool replenishment' -> 'task-system provisioning'); REQ-016 notification ('workspace pool empty / urgent replenish alert' -> 'task-system workspace unavailable'). Nothing lost: architecture-notes REQ-026 already holds the full pool mechanism (concierge pre-creates ready workspaces, no creation API, replenish below watermark default 3, empty-pool blocker, free-tier verify + paid-tier/git-based fallback). Also fixed a stale architecture-notes REQ-012 'ai4good is never a member' leftover (-> offboards volunteer + monitoring account, d51/d52). Verify: 60/60 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder review comment on REQ-026 provisioning: 'This is implementation design, this is not a prd level item at least not the details of the how to do it'

title: Adopt Option B: task-anchored NGO comment relayed onto its Linear task (pulled forward from RM-43 v1.5)
decision: Founder picked Option B for the REQ-026 task-comment integration question ('Assuming ngo sees the ongoing/queued tasks and has a real good comment, how does it integrate with the task in Linear'). The gap: the NGO comment thread (REQ-015) was project-level and decoupled from Linear tasks, so a valuable task-specific NGO comment just sat in the general thread and the volunteer had to correlate. Option B: from the read-only status panel the NGO attaches a comment to a specific task (queued or in progress); the platform POSTS it into that Linear issue as a comment attributed 'NGO (via ai4good)' - a scoped platform write extending the existing issue-create authority; it is NOT a status change, so detect-and-revert does not apply, and the NGO still never touches Linear. The volunteer sees it inline on the task and is notified; the volunteer's deliberate reply routes back to the REQ-015 thread, while routine dev-internal Linear comments never surface to the NGO. Folded requirement-level into REQ-015 (new 'Task-anchored NGO comments' bullet) + REQ-026 Model line (NGO conversation stays on the thread EXCEPT a task-anchored comment is relayed onto its task); mechanism (relay write, attribution, reply routing) in architecture-notes REQ-026. Pulls the task-anchored piece forward from RM-43; the full Slack-style real-time channel stays deferred (RM-43). Verify green.
source: founder: 'For the comment on a task prompt capture option B'

title: Attribution model spans BOTH billable surfaces; NGO-facing direct surface gets first-class project categories; all buckets reconcile to total fuel
decision: Founder: the NGO-facing page/AI that hits an upstream model must be bookkept + counted + attributed to the project too, not just volunteer build traffic. Context: REQ-009 gateway carries ONLY volunteer build traffic; NGO-facing/platform AI (Discovery REQ-004, the funded assistant REQ-033, the NGO bot) is the DIRECT billable surface calling Anthropic directly. Raw bookkeeping already existed (REQ-029: every metered AI request 'gateway or direct' emits the same audit event with project + provider cost + fuel delta; depleted-project monitor covers both surfaces incl funded Discovery), but REQ-034's attribution model was written around volunteer/task binding and did not make the direct surface a first-class ATTRIBUTED category. Fold (REQ-034): (1) attribution explicitly spans both surfaces - gateway traffic -> a task or a taskless bucket (exploration/onboarding); the direct surface -> first-class project CATEGORIES (funded Discovery, NGO assistant), so NGO-facing token consumption is counted + attributed to the project, never uncounted or folded into build burn; every metered request on either surface carries project + category and all categories reconcile to the project's total fuel. (2) Also folded the reconciling-buckets clarification (from the prior 'how is unattributed presented' review): the NGO sees burn per deliverable + per NGO-facing category in cents (no celebration), with exploration/onboarding/unattributed as their own HONEST buckets so total fuel always reconciles to visible lines - spend never hidden or falsely folded into a task; per-volunteer-per-task detail + per-project unattributed-% / binding-failure signals stay coordinator-side. Note: pre-funding Discovery is metered in Discovery credits (outside the fuel ledger, REQ-002/004) - the fuel-side direct categories cover the FUNDED Discovery + assistant. Folded into REQ-034 (pure doc) + architecture-notes REQ-034. Verify: 16776 words, 37 REQ, 60/60 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder: 'from attribution model we must have a good bookeeping and counting for attribution from the NGO facing page for the project that utilizes a upstream model so this token count will be accounted and attribuited accordingly'

title: Full-doc consistency + plain-language pass over prd-mvp (post d45-d55): stale gateway/Lovable claims fixed, no-Lovable-API bugs corrected, jargon simplified, two flows restructured
decision: Founder asked for a fresh-eyes pass: update/simplify, propose design-vs-requirement separations, clarify flows, and replace ambiguous/high-register words. FOUR BATCHES APPLIED (founder approved the three judgment calls via AskUserQuestion). (1) STALE AFTER d40-d55: Out-of-scope #1 no longer claims gateway velocity+budget caps / real-time fuel check (provider-enforced limits per d40/41); #22 'real-time gate' -> 'governance and audit on top - it does not gate'; #26 retired 'per-key caps + the fuel gate' -> provider-enforced workspace spend+rate limits; #14 no longer claims per-task Lovable metering (caps orchestrated calls estimate-based + workspace-level status read, matching REQ-021 non-goals); 'fingerprint tripwire' -> project-binding tripwire; REQ-021 'research-preview surface' -> external vendor surface (d43); Q7 rewritten resolved-in-v1 via monitoring account (d52) with the open vendor ask (service account/API-key + member API); Story-4 volunteer-flags-credits -> platform monitoring-account read with volunteer flag as fallback; Goal 3 'Anthropic float' -> pay-as-you-go usage billing (d39). (2) NO-LOVABLE-API CONSEQUENCE BUGS (same family as the d52 catch): REQ-005.5 abandonment + REQ-027 release + REQ-007 AUP all claimed automatic Lovable-workspace revocation - impossible (no member API); now: platform-controlled access (repo/keys/Linear) revokes automatically, NGO removes Lovable membership on the platform's prompt; REQ-029's 6h revocation pager scoped to platform-controlled access; Story 6 'NGO admin from creation' fixed to match REQ-008 (read+comment during build, admin at completion; code NGO-owned throughout via MIT+two-way sync). (3) JUDGMENT CALLS (founder-approved): REQ-009 'Enforcement placement' + 'Threat posture' paragraphs compressed to plain self-explanatory requirement sentences (full doctrine stays in architecture-notes Cross-cutting A); 'assimilation window' RENAMED 'candidacy window' everywhere (arch-notes keeps the alias); REQ-021 vendor role name '(editor role)' -> capability language (arch-notes records Editor as the verified mechanism). (4) PLAIN-LANGUAGE + FLOW CLARITY: orthogonal->independent (REQ-024 title/body, REQ-005.5, s8); margin->platform share everywhere (skim glossed once in exec summary); bona-fide->genuine; guided-maintenance ritual->walkthrough; conspicuously indicated->clearly flagged; role-uniform->identical for every viewer; single pane of glass->the volunteer's single operating surface; canonical->default operating environment; primes->loads; expedite->only removes the wait; non-remediable glossed; cannot churn back->cannot be edited and resubmitted; Bimodal per-task costs->wide per-task cost variation is expected; event-granular actor-attributed->real-time signals per event and per actor; backlog predating the clone->exists before any code is cloned; OAuth-scopes rationale->vendor permissions cannot allow assign-and-comment while blocking status; legacy->rare edge case; Supply Liquidity->Volunteer Supply; split workflow->build split (which parts built in Lovable vs coded); both fuel balances->the fuel balance and the Lovable credit status (REQ-013/014/s8 - Lovable is not fuel); 'via the outbox' mechanism dropped from REQ-016; NFR 'two-target deploy' -> once deployment architecture is chosen; REQ-034 aggregation sentence split. RESTRUCTURED into steps: REQ-021 Lovable setup (NGO account/billing -> membership gate -> volunteer technical setup -> platform validation) and REQ-005.5 kickoff sequence (bulleted parallel side effects). Verify: 16910 words, 37 REQ, 60/60 RM bijection, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned words, 0 FFFD. ~45 edits across all 9 pure-s* files + architecture-notes (alias + Editor-mechanism note).
source: founder (model switched to Fable 5): 'go over the MVP PRD and the transcripts for what we have done lately using opus 4.8 and update and simplify where possible...' + AskUserQuestion answers: REQ-009 'make wording more simple and self explanatory'; candidacy window; capability language

title: Skill-side Lovable budget guardrails REMOVED + NGO orchestration consent gate REMOVED; sole spend bound = NGO-set native Lovable credit cap
decision: Two founder review cuts on REQ-028. (1) 'no guardrails': the Skill-side Lovable budget machinery is removed - no per-task prompt cap, no interactive confirmation with burn estimates, no refusal beyond an NGO-set hard cap, no estimate-based 'Lovable burn' on dashboards. The volunteer's Lovable spend is bounded ONLY by the NGO-set per-member credit cap native to Lovable (d46/d47 monthly_credit_limit), and visibility is the workspace-level credit status the platform's monitoring account reads (d50-d52). Orchestrated calls stay audit-logged. Rationale: provider-native limits over platform-reconstructed estimates - the exact d40 pattern applied to the Lovable meter (d40 retired custom gateway caps in favor of Anthropic workspace spend/rate limits). (2) 'not needed': the NGO consent gate ('Allow Skill to orchestrate Lovable' - on by default, revocable, checked before every call) is removed - orchestration is inherent to the mandatory Lovable build path (every project uses Lovable, the NGO owns and funds the workspace, cost is disclosed at kickoff), so a per-call consent check is friction with no decision behind it. Edits: REQ-028 orchestration-shell section (guardrails bullet replaced with the no-guardrails statement; consent-gate bullet deleted); REQ-021 orchestration posture ('under a per-task Lovable-credit cap' removed; spend bounded only by the NGO-set native cap, no platform-side caps); Story 4 ('under per-task Lovable-credit budget caps' removed); Out-of-scope #14 (platform 'never meters or caps Lovable usage'); architecture-notes REQ-028 (guardrails struck through + consent-gate removal recorded). The match consent gates (REQ-007, volunteer confirm + NGO acknowledgment) are UNRELATED and stay. Verify: 16904 words, 37 REQ, 60/60 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder review comments on REQ-028: budget guardrails quote -> 'no guardrails'; consent gate quote -> 'not needed'

title: Orchestration actor corrected: the VOLUNTEER drives Lovable via Lovable's own MCP; the Skill is conventions+helpers, never an orchestration engine; 'replaceable integration layer' framing dropped
decision: Founder review on REQ-028's orchestration-shell bullet ('It drives Lovable through the replaceable integration layer... Per task it recommends build-locally vs delegate-to-Lovable'): 'this is not the skill - its run by the volunteer dev using lovable mcp'. Correction folded: orchestration is RUN BY THE VOLUNTEER from their Claude Code session directly over Lovable's own MCP; the Skill ships conventions, context, and helper flows but is not the actor and does not recommend/decide - the build-locally vs delegate-to-Lovable choice is the volunteer's, with Discovery's build split as guidance, never binding. This also dissolves the 'replaceable integration layer' framing (the old LovableDriver anti-corruption-port idea): there is NO ai4good-owned driver layer between the volunteer and Lovable - the vendor's MCP is the connection, and the breakage fallback is the volunteer driving Lovable in-browser (both paths commit to the shared repo), which preserves the requirement-level guarantee (a Lovable break degrades to manual work, never a dead build) without inventing platform infrastructure. Edits: REQ-028 section retitled 'Lovable orchestration (v1 core)' with the actor corrected; REQ-021 orchestration posture ('accessed through a replaceable integration layer' -> 'driven from the volunteer's Claude Code session via Lovable's own MCP'); Story 4 ('The Skill drives Lovable' -> 'The volunteer drives Lovable from Claude Code via Lovable's MCP'; 'connect ... Lovable orchestration' -> 'the Lovable MCP'); s8 scope index REQ-021 (drops 'behind a replaceable integration layer', also fixes stale 'manual status' -> monitoring-account read w/ manual fallback) + REQ-028 ('+ the Lovable orchestration layer' -> 'the volunteer drives Lovable via its MCP'); architecture-notes REQ-021 ('one replaceable internal adapter' struck through, d58 note). Verify: 16933 words, 37 REQ, 60/60 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder review comment on REQ-028: 'this is not the skill suggests its run by the volunteer dev using loveable mcp'

title: REQ-029 observability/monitoring framework KILLED from MVP -> RM-63; audit event re-homed to REQ-034, money dashboard to REQ-030; 'pages' language downgraded to dashboard+notification
decision: Founder reviewed REQ-029 (explained: backend watchdogs paging the founder; only visible artifact = the admin-only money dashboard; NGO/volunteer see nothing from it) and ruled 'this should be out of scope from the mvp'. Folded as a kill-with-untangling: (1) REQ-029 -> deferred stub (heading kept for the 37-REQ count): no job heartbeats/watchdog, no business-invariant pagers (negative balance, billable request on a depleted project, revocation completeness, org permission drift, error budgets), no operator paging; v1 detection = founder's daily money-dashboard read + NFR error-spike alerts + automatic provider-truth reconciliation; risk accepted at pilot scale (silently-failed job or money-path bug caught by the daily read or user report, not machinery). New RM-63 carries the full framework; trigger = before organic/public volume or the first silent automation failure. (2) LOAD-BEARING PIECES RE-HOMED, not killed: the per-request privacy-preserving audit event (metadata only, never bodies) moved into REQ-034 as the attribution/reconciliation data spine (REQ-034's cross-ref '(REQ-029)' removed); the one money dashboard (funding/consumption/share/reconciliation/chargebacks, founder-read daily, RM-34) moved into REQ-030. (3) 'PAGES' LANGUAGE DOWNGRADED everywhere to what exists without a paging framework: REQ-030 refusal-to-guess backstop -> undecidable drift surfaces on the money dashboard + notifies the platform admin (REQ-016); REQ-030 'large drift pages' -> notifies admin; REQ-006 'only undecidable drift pages' -> 'needs a human - surfaced to the platform admin (REQ-030)'; s9 risk 'undecidable-cases page' -> 'surface to the founder (dashboard + notification)'; s7 NFR reliability line drops gateway operator-paging, keeps centralized errors/logs + error-spike alerts, points to RM-63. NOTE: the 20/5/0% fuel-threshold monitor is UNTOUCHED - it is REQ-009 product behavior (NGO warnings), not observability. Promise 8 ('only undecidable drift surfacing') already framework-neutral - unchanged. Arch-notes REQ-029/030 section updated (framework = RM-63 design). Verify: 16982 words, 37 REQ headings, 61/61 RM bijection (RM-63 added), 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder: 'revisit req 29 and explain it and justify or kill it' -> after the panel-audience clarification (admin-only pager + one admin dashboard): 'this should be out of scope from the mvp'

decision: Founder: 'what is deferred from mvp prd shouldn't be there - should be on the [roadmap] doc'. Removed all seven deferred stubs from prd-mvp.md: REQ-017 (post-completion feature requests, v2), REQ-018 (voice input), REQ-019 (multi-volunteer), REQ-020 (public impact page), REQ-022 (volunteer tips), REQ-029 (observability framework, killed d59), REQ-035 (post-completion attribution & health, deferred d45) - plus their two container sections ('Out of v1 / Deferred to v2' and 'Nice to Have (P2)'). Every deferral already has its full record in roadmap.md (RM-4, RM-54, RM-13, RM-55, RM-11, RM-63, RM-62 respectively), so nothing was lost. UNTANGLING: (1) RM-54 and RM-55 were referenced ONLY by the removed stubs -> relocated to s8's 'Additional post-MVP items with no other v1-doc mention' pointer line (bijection preserved). (2) REQ-029's one live v1 fact folded into REQ-030 as a single line (no monitoring framework in v1 -> RM-63; detection = daily dashboard read + error-spike alerts + automatic reconciliation; risk accepted). (3) All dangling references to removed IDs scrubbed: s7 NFR '(REQ-029 -> RM-63)' -> '(-> RM-63)'; out-of-scope #12 'tips (REQ-022)' -> 'tips (post-MVP -> RM-11)'; s8 v1 list dropped the 'REQ-022 -' prefix and the whole REQ-035 deferral line (a v1 scope list should not list deferred items); the P0-promoted intro 'REQ-017 is out of v1' -> 'Post-completion feature-request surfacing is out of v1 (-> RM-4)'. (4) Reader note added to the s8 scope intro: 'REQ IDs are stable identifiers: an ID absent from this document is a deferred requirement tracked in roadmap.md (RM-N)' - this also answers the founder's earlier REQ-numbering-gap question in-doc. Assembled-doc grep for the seven removed IDs: zero matches. Verify: 16807 words, 30 REQ headings (was 37; the verify baseline is now 30), 61/61 RM bijection, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.

title: Attribution recording clarified: ONE append-only usage log with two in-line writers, not an event system; REQ-034 'emits audit event' language replaced
decision: Founder challenged the REQ-034 audit-event line (re-homed from killed REQ-029 in d59): 'how attribution works? firing events all over this sounds bad'. Clarification: the mechanism was never an event architecture - 'emits an audit event' was misdescribing a log-table INSERT. Actual mechanism: (volunteer path) self-assign -> the Skill writes the task ID per worktree (the binding) -> the binding rides each gateway request -> the gateway, while proxying the request it ALREADY handles, records one row in a single append-only usage log (project from the key; task from the binding, or the exploration/onboarding/unattributed floor; provider cost/status); (direct path) the platform backend records the same row for its own Discovery/assistant calls inside the same function that already meters the turn against fuel. TWO writers total, both components already on the money path; no event bus, no subscribers, no fan-out, no reactive consumers; attribution views, NGO burn buckets, and reconciliation are SQL reads over that one table computed on view; the log never gates a request (REQ-034 telemetry ceiling unchanged). Note: the fuel 20/5/0% thresholds read the provider Admin API (d41), NOT this log - the log is fully passive. REQ-034 bullet reworded to state the shape as constraints: 'One usage log, not an event system' - single row per metered request, written in-line by the component already handling that request, one append-only log, reads-only downstream, never gates. Architecture-notes REQ-034 gains the one-line end-to-end mechanism. REQ-016's notification event taxonomy is a separate, deliberate event design (notifications ARE events) - untouched. Verify: 16862 words, 30 REQ, 61/61 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder review comment on REQ-034: 'how attribution works ? firing events all over this sounds bad'

title: Future content-forensics question recorded: if disputes ever need prompt-level evidence, the shape is prospective incident-triggered preservation (litigation hold) - retroactive forensics is structurally incompatible with the permanently-out content posture; v1 unchanged
decision: Founder confirmed the attribution model (gateway writes metadata rows for volunteer traffic - correct, with the nuance that the platform backend writes rows for its own Discovery/assistant calls) and raised: 'later if system succeeds we might need to store the actual data log for forensics of incidents if allegations between parties occur'. Recorded, NOT folded into v1: (1) metadata + public artifacts already cover the realistic dispute surface without bodies - misdirected-fuel claims (attribution buckets, unattributed-%, tripwire mismatch flags, timing/volume, per-task costs), bad-code claims (the public repo, commits/PRs, Lovable edit history, Linear history), value-for-money claims (burn-per-deliverable, Promise 4); prompt bodies mainly add INTENT evidence. (2) The hard either/or, on record: RETROACTIVE content forensics requires always-on storage of all prompts - directly amending Out-of-scope #21 ('permanently out... deliberate and on record'), rewriting the volunteer disclosure (metadata 'attributed and reviewed') into content-capture consent, taking on GDPR/data-minimization + breach-target exposure, and spending a volunteer-trust/recruiting asset. (3) If ever needed, the workable shape is PROSPECTIVE incident-triggered preservation (litigation-hold model): default never-store; on a formally opened dispute, preservation starts from that moment, scoped to that project, with notice to both parties, time-bounded, counsel-reviewed; retroactivity is deliberately given up - that IS the privacy guarantee. Deliberately NOT an RM item: the permanently-out list exists to prevent re-litigation, so amending it is a founder-level posture decision outside the roadmap. Captured in architecture-notes REQ-034 storage/leakage map. v1 PRD unchanged.
source: founder: 'the gateway store the log metadata and that is the basis for the attribution counter of dev work. later if system succedd we might need to store the actual data log for forensics of incidents if allegation between parties will occur'

title: Token-native attribution: fuel/money is dollar-based (provider billed cost), attribution metrics are TOKEN-based; dollar views derive at read time scaled to the billed total
decision: Founder, following the what-does-Anthropic-echo clarification (provider echoes token counts per response, never dollars): 'fuel is money based but attribution metrics should be based in tokens'. Folded into REQ-034: (1) the STORED attribution metric is the provider's echoed token counts per task/bucket/category - facts that never change; (2) dollar views DERIVE at read time: each bucket's token categories priced by the versioned rate card, then all buckets scaled proportionally to sum exactly to the workspace's billed cost (REQ-006 provider truth) - so the rate card affects only the split and the billed total always wins; a rate-card change re-prices a view, never rewrites recorded attribution; (3) per-task burn baselines are token-based (stable across price changes, comparable across time); (4) the NGO's burn-per-deliverable display stays in cents (NGOs think in money) but is explicitly derived-from-token-attribution-scaled-to-billed. Benefits: price changes never corrupt attribution history; task baselines survive repricing; displayed cents always reconcile to real money; consistency with the already-token-based Discovery credit unit (d11/d13) - the whole measurement stack is token-native with money entering only at the fuel boundary. Mechanism note (arch-notes): category weighting is load-bearing in the derivation (cache reads ~10x cheaper than fresh input; raw token-share allocation would distort cache-heavy tasks); the per-row computed-cost column is a derived convenience, never the metric. Edits: REQ-034 new tokens-are-the-metric bullet + aggregation-boundary line notes the derivation; arch-notes REQ-034 allocation mechanism. Verify: 16977 words, 30 REQ, 61/61 RM, 0 decision-NN, 0 banned, 0 FFFD.
source: founder: 'Yes so fuel is money based but atrribution metrics should've based in tokens'
